evals.name: outline-seminar
evals.prompt:
I need to prepare a 20-minute seminar for my CS reading group next week on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). I have a rough outline:

- What RAG is and why it exists (LLM knowledge cutoffs, hallucination)
- Architecture: retriever + reader, embeddings, vector DBs
- Three main flavors: naive RAG, advanced RAG (re-ranking, query rewriting), agentic RAG
- Limitations: retrieval failures, context-window bloat, evaluation is hard
- Where it's going next

My name is Adam Mikol. Audience is mixed grad students. Light theme, default colors are fine. Make me both the slide deck and a script I can actually read off when I'm presenting.
evals.expected_output: An HTML deck (~12-14 slides) following the narrative arc, a script.md with teleprompter-style spoken text and transitions, a README explaining what was made, and the supporting JS/JSX files. Customizations should match defaults (light, default accent).

assertions.description: An HTML deck file is produced in the output folder
A script.md file is produced alongside the HTML
deck-stage.js, image-slot.js, and tweaks-panel.jsx are copied alongside the HTML
Slide count is between 11 and 16 (appropriate for 20 min)
Deck content actually covers RAG, retrievers, vector DBs, and the three flavors mentioned
Cover slide and footers show 'Adam Mikol', not 'Your Name' placeholder
Every slide's NN/NN page number matches its position and the total count
Script uses short sentences, contractions, transitions between slides — not just bullet-style summaries
Script length is within 20% of 2800 words (20 min at ~140 wpm)
Deck follows situation → complication → resolution structure, not paper-style sections

evals.name: paper-condense
evals.prompt:
I have to give a 15-minute talk at my journal club next Thursday about this short paper:

---
Title: "Attention Is Not All You Need: Pure MLP Models Match Transformers on Vision Tasks"
(hypothetical paper for the test)

Key claims:
- Transformer attention isn't necessary for strong image classification — a carefully designed MLP-only architecture (MLP-Mixer) reaches ViT-Base accuracy on ImageNet (76.4% vs 77.9%)
- Uses two MLP types: token-mixing MLPs (across spatial locations) and channel-mixing MLPs
- Trains 2.5x faster than ViT-Base at equivalent accuracy
- But: needs JFT-300M pretraining to close the gap; on ImageNet-only training, ViT still wins by ~3 points
- Authors argue this is evidence that the inductive bias of attention is less important than commonly believed

My take: I think the result is real but oversold — they need 300M pretraining images to match a model trained on 14M. That's not 'attention is not needed', that's 'attention is data-efficient'.

I'm Marie Chen. The journal club is small (8 people), informal but technical. Make it serious-looking. Dark mode please, accent should be something that feels modern — maybe an electric blue or coral. Compact density since we're going to argue over every slide.
evals.expected_output: An HTML deck targeted for 15 min (~10-12 slides), dark mode body class, compact density, an electric blue or coral accent override, content that reflects both the paper's claims AND the speaker's contrarian take (this isn't a neutral summary). Script.md in teleprompter style that voices the speaker's argument.

assertions.description: An HTML deck file is produced
A script.md is produced
body element has class 'dark'
body element has class 'compact'
--accent CSS variable changed from default #2a5d8a to electric blue or coral hex
Slide count between 9 and 13
The speaker's contrarian take (attention is data-efficient, not unnecessary) appears in the deck OR script, not just the paper's claims
Cover shows 'Marie Chen'
Script reads as spoken text with transitions, not as paragraph summary
Page numbers are correctly renumbered to match total

evals.name: lightning-talk
evals.prompt: 5 min lightning talk for a Friday demo at work. Topic: my weekend hack — a CLI tool that watches a directory for new screenshots and auto-OCRs them into a markdown notebook. I want it punchy and visually striking. Spacious density, light mode, accent in warm coral. Use Space Grotesk for the display font. Speaker: Jamie. No script needed for this one, just the deck — I'll wing the talking.
evals.expected_output: A short HTML deck (~5-6 slides) with spacious density, light mode, --accent set to a coral hex, --font-display swapped to Space Grotesk, no section dividers, lean structure. Should respect the user's 'no script' request — produce the deck only, with maybe a note acknowledging the choice.

assertions.description: An HTML deck file is produced
body has class 'spacious'
body does NOT have class 'dark'
--accent CSS variable changed to a coral hex (warm orange-red range)
--font-display value contains 'Space Grotesk'
Total slide count is between 4 and 7
Deck does not include section-divider slides (inappropriate for 5-min talk)
Either no script.md was created, or the assistant explicitly noted skipping it per the user's request
Cover shows 'Jamie' as the speaker
